actions.then: also provide user_provided_headers: — 1.1.0 now raises an error if you set the former without the latter.

actions.if: your CSV files have duplicate header names
actions.then: rename the duplicates, or be ready to rescue the new validation error — 1.2.0 added default validation that each header appears only once.
switch hash values to symbols (or update downstream reads to use string keys) — 1.3.0 stopped silently coercing values to symbols.

actions.if: you relied on lines starting with # being treated as comments
actions.then: pass comment_regexp: /\A#/ explicitly — 1.5.0 changed the default to nil.

actions.if: you rescue an exception when key_mapping: has an unused key
actions.then: remove that rescue clause — 1.6.1 changed this from an exception to a warning.

actions.if: you accept CSV files from users or other external sources where the column separator might not be a comma (e.g. locale-specific exports using ; or tab), or where a file might have only one column
actions.then: wrap your SmarterCSV.process calls in rescue SmarterCSV::NoColSepDetected — 1.8.0 made col_sep: :auto and row_sep: :auto the new defaults.
rename it to SmarterCSV::MissingKeys — 1.9.0 renamed the error.

actions.if: you use key_mapping: and want to allow some mapped headers to be missing
actions.then: pass silence_missing_keys: true — 1.9.0 now raises MissingKeys for unmapped headers (used to silently print).
write the list in the exact final form you want (all symbols or all strings) — 1.10.0 stopped applying additional transformations. strings_as_keys: is ignored alongside it.

actions.if: you depended on duplicate-header detection failing fast
actions.then: pass duplicate_header_suffix: nil explicitly — 1.10.0 changed the default to '' (it auto-disambiguates duplicates as name, name2, ...).

actions.if: you call SmarterCSV.process and don't need to inspect headers / warnings / errors after parsing
actions.then: no code changes needed — SmarterCSV.process is backwards-compatible. (The 1.11 class-level accessors SmarterCSV.headers / SmarterCSV.raw_header are gone in 1.12.0 — if you used those, see the next question.)

actions.if: you want access to parsed headers / warnings / errors after processing, OR you process CSV files in threaded code (Puma workers, Sidekiq jobs, or any concurrent context)
actions.then: use the new Reader API: reader = SmarterCSV::Reader.new(file, options); data = reader.process; headers = reader.headers; warnings = reader.warnings. 1.12.0 introduced SmarterCSV::Reader with thread-safe per-instance state, replacing the class-level accessors that existed in 1.11.

actions.if: your CSV rows can have more columns than the header AND your code expects only header-listed keys
actions.then: filter out the new auto-generated :column_N keys, or pass strict: true to raise on extras — 1.13.0 keeps extra columns instead of dropping them silently.

actions.if: any of your input files might have unbalanced quotes
actions.then: wrap calls in rescue SmarterCSV::MalformedCSV — 1.13.0 now raises instead of producing garbled output.

actions.if: you pass user_provided_headers: AND your file has a header line that should be skipped
actions.then: also pass headers_in_file: true explicitly — 1.13.0 made user_provided_headers: imply headers_in_file: false by default.

actions.if: your Ruby version is 2.5 or older
actions.then:
upgrade Ruby to 2.6 or newer — 1.15.0 dropped support for Ruby 2.5.

The migration is small: Ruby 2.5 reached end-of-life in March 2021 (no more security fixes anywhere), and Ruby 2.5 → 2.6 is API-compatible for nearly all code. Update your .ruby-version or the ruby line in your Gemfile, run bundle install, and you're done. Most users jump straight to a current Ruby (3.x).

actions.if: your CSV files contain stray " characters in the middle of unquoted fields
actions.then: verify the output is now correct — 1.16.0 treats them as literal (RFC 4180). Output gets more correct for almost everyone; the temporary escape hatch quote_boundary: :legacy exists if your downstream code depended on the previously-corrupted output (not recommended for new code).
